listitem.para:
BCR[7:6]: Device Role
0: I3C Target
1: I3C Controller capable
2: Reserved
3: Reserved

BCR[5]: Advanced Capabilities
0: Does not support optional advanced capabilities.
1: Supports optional advanced capabilities which can be viewed via GETCAPS CCC.

BCR[4]: Virtual Target Support
0: Is not a virtual target.
1: Is a virtual target.

BCR[3]: Offline Capable
0: Will always response to I3C commands.
1: Will not always response to I3C commands.

BCR[2]: IBI Payload
0: No data bytes following the accepted IBI.
1: One data byte (MDB, Mandatory Data Byte) follows the accepted IBI. Additional data bytes may also follows.

BCR[1]: IBI Request Capable
0: Not capable
1: Capable

BCR[0]: Max Data Speed Limitation
0: No Limitation
1: Limitation obtained via GETMXDS CCC.

briefdescription.para: Max Data Speed Limitation bit.

detaileddescription.para: 0 - No Limitation. 1 - Limitation obtained via GETMXDS CCC.

briefdescription.para: IBI Request Capable bit.
IBI Payload bit.

detaileddescription.para: 0 - No data bytes following the accepted IBI. 1 - One data byte (MDB, Mandatory Data Byte) follows the accepted IBI. Additional data bytes may also follows.

briefdescription.para: Offline Capable bit.

detaileddescription.para: 0 - Will always respond to I3C commands. 1 - Will not always respond to I3C commands.

briefdescription.para: Virtual Target Support bit.

detaileddescription.para: 0 - Is not a virtual target. 1 - Is a virtual target.

briefdescription.para: Advanced Capabilities bit.

detaileddescription.para: 0 - Does not support optional advanced capabilities. 1 - Supports optional advanced capabilities which can be viewed via GETCAPS CCC.

briefdescription.para: Device Role - I3C Target.
Device Role - I3C Controller Capable.
Device Role bit shift mask.
Device Role.

detaileddescription.para: Obtain Device Role value from the BCR value obtained via GETBCR.

description.para:
Legacy Virtual Register (LVR)
LVR[7:5]: I2C device index:
0: I2C device has a 50 ns spike filter where it is not affected by high frequency on SCL.
1: I2C device does not have a 50 ns spike filter but can work with high frequency on SCL.
2: I2C device does not have a 50 ns spike filter and cannot work with high frequency on SCL.
LVR[4]: I2C mode indicator:
0: FM+ mode
1: FM mode
LVR[3:0]: Reserved.

briefdescription.para: I2C FM+ Mode.
I2C FM Mode.
I2C Mode Indicator bitmask.
I2C Mode.

detaileddescription.para: Obtain I2C Mode value from the LVR value.

briefdescription.para: I2C Device Index 0.

detaileddescription.para: I2C device has a 50 ns spike filter where it is not affected by high frequency on SCL.

briefdescription.para: I2C Device Index 1.

detaileddescription.para: I2C device does not have a 50 ns spike filter but can work with high frequency on SCL.

briefdescription.para: I2C Device Index 2.

detaileddescription.para: I2C device does not have a 50 ns spike filter and cannot work with high frequency on SCL.

briefdescription.para: I2C Device Index bitmask.
I2C Device Index.

detaileddescription.para: Obtain I2C Device Index value from the LVR value.

briefdescription.para: Only I3C devices are on the bus.
Both I3C and legacy I2C devices are on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: The I2C devices have 50ns spike filter on SCL.

briefdescription.para: Both I3C and legacy I2C devices are on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: The I2C devices do not have 50ns spike filter on SCL and can tolerate maximum SDR SCL clock frequency.

briefdescription.para: Both I3C and legacy I2C devices are on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: The I2C devices do not have 50ns spike filter on SCL but cannot tolerate maximum SDR SCL clock frequency.

briefdescription.para: I3C bus mode.
I2C FM mode.
I2C FM+ mode.
I2C bus speed under I3C bus.

detaileddescription.para: Only FM and FM+ modes are supported for I2C devices under I3C bus.

briefdescription.para: Single Data Rate messaging.
High Data Rate - Double Data Rate messaging.
High Data Rate - Ternary Symbol Legacy-inclusive-Bus.
High Data Rate - Ternary Symbol for Pure Bus.
High Data Rate - Bulk Transport.
I3C data rate.

detaileddescription.para: I3C data transfer rate defined by the I3C specification.

briefdescription.para: Type of configuration being passed to configure function.
Find a I3C target device descriptor by ID.

detaileddescription.para: This finds the I3C target device descriptor in the device list matching the provided ID struct (id).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device list struct.
Pointer to I3C device ID struct.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the I3C target device descriptor, or NULL if none is found.

briefdescription.para: Find a I3C target device descriptor by dynamic address.

detaileddescription.para: This finds the I3C target device descriptor in the attached device list matching the dynamic address (addr)

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the I3C target device descriptor, or NULL if none is found.

briefdescription.para: Find a I3C target device descriptor by static address.

detaileddescription.para: This finds the I3C target device descriptor in the attached device list matching the static address (addr)

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the I3C target device descriptor, or NULL if none is found.

briefdescription.para: Find a I2C target device descriptor by address.

detaileddescription.para: This finds the I2C target device descriptor in the attached device list matching the address (addr)

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the I2C target device descriptor, or NULL if none is found.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct i3c_addr_slots *addr_slots, const struct i3c_dev_list *dev_list, uint64_t pid, bool must_match, bool assigned_okay, struct i3c_device_desc **target, uint8_t *addr)
memberdef.name: i3c_dev_list_daa_addr_helper

briefdescription.para: Helper function to find a usable address during ENTDAA.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper function to find a usable address during Dynamic Address Assignment. Given the PID (pid), it will search through the device list for the matching device descriptor. If the device descriptor indicates that there is a preferred address (i.e. assigned-address in device tree, i3c_device_desc::init_dynamic_addr), this preferred address will be returned if this address is still available. If it is not available, another free address will be returned.
If must_match is true, the PID (pid) must match one of the device in the device list.

If must_match is false, this will return an arbitrary address. This is useful when not all devices are described in device tree. Or else, the DAA process cannot proceed since there is no address to be assigned.

If assigned_okay is true, it will return the same address already assigned to the device (i3c_device_desc::dynamic_addr). If no address has been assigned, it behaves as if assigned_okay is false. This is useful for assigning the same address to the same device (for example, hot-join after device coming back from suspend).
If assigned_okay is false, the device cannot have an address assigned already (that i3c_device_desc::dynamic_addr is not zero). This is mainly used during the initial DAA.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device list struct.
Provisioned ID of device to be assigned address.
True if PID must match devices in the device list. False otherwise.
True if it is okay to return the address already assigned to the target matching the PID (pid).
Store the pointer of the device descriptor if it matches the incoming PID (pid).
Address to be assigned to target device.
if no device matches the PID (pid) in the device list and must_match is true.
if the device matching PID (pid) already has an address assigned or invalid function arguments.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum i3c_config_type type, void *config)
memberdef.name: i3c_configure

briefdescription.para: Configure the I3C hardware.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.
Type of configuration parameters being passed in config.

briefdescription.para: Get the controller device configuration for an I3C device.

detaileddescription.para: This function retrieves the configuration parameters specific to an I3C controller device. It is a type-safe wrapper around i3c_config_get that ensures the correct structure type is passed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the I3C controller device instance.
Pointer to a i3c_config_controller structure where the configuration will be stored.

briefdescription.para: Get the target device configuration for an I3C device.

detaileddescription.para: This function retrieves the configuration parameters specific to an I3C target device. It is a type-safe wrapper around i3c_config_get that ensures the correct structure type is passed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the I3C controller device instance.
Pointer to a i3c_config_target structure where the configuration will be stored.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum i3c_config_type type, void *config)
memberdef.name: i3c_config_get

briefdescription.para: Get configuration of the I3C hardware.

detaileddescription.para: This provides a way to get the current configuration of the I3C hardware.
This can return cached config or probed hardware parameters, but it has to be up to date with current configuration.

dev
Pointer to controller device driver instance.
type
Type of configuration parameters being passed in config.
config
Pointer to the configuration parameters.
Note that if type is I3C_CONFIG_CUSTOM, config must contain the ID of the parameter to be retrieved.

briefdescription.para: Get the controller device configuration for an I3C device.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the configuration parameters specific to an I3C controller device. It is a type-safe wrapper around i3c_config_get that ensures the correct structure type is passed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the I3C controller device instance.
Pointer to a i3c_config_controller structure where the configuration will be used.

briefdescription.para: Get the target device configuration for an I3C device.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the configuration parameters specific to an I3C target device. It is a type-safe wrapper around i3c_config_get that ensures the correct structure type is passed.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the I3C controller device instance.
Pointer to a i3c_config_target structure where the configuration will be used.

briefdescription.para: Attempt bus recovery on the I3C bus.

detaileddescription.para: This routine asks the controller to attempt bus recovery.

parameterdescription.para: Bus recovery is not supported by the controller driver.

briefdescription.para: Attach an I3C device.

detaileddescription.para: Called to attach a I3C device to the addresses. This is typically called before a SETDASA or ENTDAA to reserve the addresses. This will also call the optional api to update any registers within the driver if implemented.

simplesect.para: Use cases involving multiple writers to the i3c/i2c devices must prevent concurrent write operations, either by preventing all writers from being preempted or by using a mutex to govern writes to the i3c/i2c devices.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor
If address is not available or if the device has already been attached before

briefdescription.para: Reattach I3C device.

detaileddescription.para: called after every time an I3C device has its address changed. It can be because the device has been powered down and has lost its address, or it can happen when a device had a static address and has been assigned a dynamic address with SETDASA or a dynamic address has been updated with SETNEWDA. This will also call the optional api to update any registers within the driver if implemented.

simplesect.para: Use cases involving multiple writers to the i3c/i2c devices must prevent concurrent write operations, either by preventing all writers from being preempted or by using a mutex to govern writes to the i3c/i2c devices.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor
The old dynamic address of target device, 0 if there was no old dynamic address

briefdescription.para: Detach I3C Device.

detaileddescription.para: called to remove an I3C device and to free up the address that it used. If it's dynamic address was not set, then it assumed that SETDASA failed and will free it's static addr. This will also call the optional api to update any registers within the driver if implemented.

simplesect.para: Use cases involving multiple writers to the i3c/i2c devices must prevent concurrent write operations, either by preventing all writers from being preempted or by using a mutex to govern writes to the i3c/i2c devices.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor

briefdescription.para: Attach an I2C device.

detaileddescription.para: Called to attach a I2C device to the addresses. This will also call the optional api to update any registers within the driver if implemented.

simplesect.para: Use cases involving multiple writers to the i3c/i2c devices must prevent concurrent write operations, either by preventing all writers from being preempted or by using a mutex to govern writes to the i3c/i2c devices.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor
If address is not available or if the device has already been attached before

briefdescription.para: Detach I2C Device.

detaileddescription.para: called to remove an I2C device and to free up the address that it used. This will also call the optional api to update any registers within the driver if implemented.

simplesect.para: Use cases involving multiple writers to the i3c/i2c devices must prevent concurrent write operations, either by preventing all writers from being preempted or by using a mutex to govern writes to the i3c/i2c devices.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor

briefdescription.para: Perform Dynamic Address Assignment on the I3C bus.

detaileddescription.para: This routine asks the controller to perform dynamic address assignment where the controller belongs. Only the active controller of the bus should do this.

simplesect.para: For controller driver implementation, the controller should perform SETDASA to allow static addresses to be the dynamic addresses before actually doing ENTDAA.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the controller driver instance.
If a provisioned ID does not match to any target devices in the registered device list.
No more free addresses can be assigned to target.
Dynamic address assignment is not supported by the controller driver.

briefdescription.para: Send CCC to the bus.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the controller driver instance.
Pointer to the structure describing the CCC payload.
Invalid valid set in the payload structure.

briefdescription.para: Find a registered I3C target device.

detaileddescription.para: Controller only API.
This returns the I3C device descriptor of the I3C device matching the incoming id.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.

simplesect.para: Pointer to I3C device descriptor, or NULL if no I3C device found matching incoming id.

briefdescription.para: Generic helper function to perform bus initialization.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.
If a provisioned ID does not match to any target devices in the registered device list.
No more free addresses can be assigned to target.
Dynamic address assignment is not supported by the controller driver.

briefdescription.para: Get basic information from device and update device descriptor.

detaileddescription.para:
This retrieves some basic information:
Bus Characteristics Register (GETBCR)
Device Characteristics Register (GETDCR) from the device and update the corresponding fields of the device descriptor.
This only updates the field(s) in device descriptor only if CCC operations succeed.

briefdescription.para: Get advanced information from device and update device descriptor.

detaileddescription.para:
This retrieves some information:
Max Read Length (GETMRL)
Max Write Length (GETMWL)
Get Capabilities (GETCAPS)
Max Device Speed (GETMXDS) (if applicable) from the device and update the corresponding fields of the device descriptor.
This only updates the field(s) in device descriptor only if CCC operations succeed.

simplesect.para: This should only be called after i3c_device_basic_info_get() or if the BCR was already obtained through ENTDAA, DEFTGTS, or GETBCR.

briefdescription.para: Get all information from device and update device descriptor.

detaileddescription.para:
This retrieves all information:
Bus Characteristics Register (GETBCR)
Device Characteristics Register (GETDCR)
Max Read Length (GETMRL)
Max Write Length (GETMWL)
Get Capabilities (GETCAPS)
Max Device Speed (GETMXDS) (if applicable) from the device and update the corresponding fields of the device descriptor.
This only updates the field(s) in device descriptor only if CCC operations succeed.

briefdescription.para: Determine I3C bus mode from the I2C devices on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: Reads the LVR of all I2C devices and returns the I3C bus mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device list struct.

briefdescription.para: Check if the bus has a secondary controller.

detaileddescription.para: This reads the BCR from the device descriptor struct of all targets to determine whether a device is a secondary controller.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.

simplesect.para: True if the bus has a secondary controller, false otherwise.

briefdescription.para: Reset all devices on the bus and clear their dynamic addresses.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the RSTDAA (Reset Dynamic Address Assignment) CCC to all devices on the bus.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the controller device driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Assign a dynamic address to a device using its static address.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the SETDASA (Set Dynamic Address from Static Address) CCC to the target device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.
The dynamic address to assign to the device.
If the address is not available.

briefdescription.para: Assign a new dynamic address to a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the SETNEWDA (Set New Dynamic Address) CCC to the target device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.
The new dynamic address to assign to the device.
If the address is not available.

briefdescription.para: Assign static addresses as dynamic addresses for all devices on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the SETAASA (Set All Addresses to Static Address) CCC to all devices on the bus.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the controller device driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Bus Characteristics Register (BCR) of a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the GETBCR CCC to the target device and updates its descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Device Characteristics Register (DCR) of a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the GETDCR CCC to the target device and updates its descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Provisional ID (PID) of a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the GETPID CCC to the target device and updates its descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Maximum Read Length (MRL) of a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the GETMRL CCC to the target device and updates its descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Maximum Write Length (MWL) of a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the GETMWL CCC to the target device and updates its descriptor.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

briefdescription.para: Set the Maximum Read Length (MRL) for a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the SETMRL CCC to the target device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

briefdescription.para: Set the Maximum Write Length (MWL) for a device.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the SETMWL CCC to the target device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint16_t mrl, uint8_t ibi_len, bool has_ibi_size)
memberdef.name: i3c_bus_setmrl_all

briefdescription.para: Set the Maximum Read Length (MRL) for all devices on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the SETMRL CCC to all devices on the bus.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the controller device driver instance.
True if to transmit max ibi len

briefdescription.para: Set the Maximum Write Length (MWL) for all devices on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: Sends the SETMWL CCC to all devices on the bus.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the controller device driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Retrieve the Active Controller's Dynamic Address (ACCCR).

detaileddescription.para: Sends the GETACCCR CCC to the target device and verifies the response.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the target device descriptor.

briefdescription.para: Send the CCC DEFTGTS.

detaileddescription.para: This builds the payload required for DEFTGTS and transmits it out

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to controller device driver instance.
No memory to build the payload.

briefdescription.para: Calculate odd parity.

detaileddescription.para: Calculate the Odd Parity of a Target Address.

briefdescription.para: Perform Controller Handoff.

detaileddescription.para: This performs the controller handoff according to 5.1.7.1 of I3C v1.1.1 Specification.

parameterdescription.para: True if the target requested the Handoff, False if the active controller is passing it to a secondary controller

briefdescription.para: Call back for when Controllership is Handoffed to itself.

detaileddescription.para: This reads the DEFTGTS it received earlier and processes it by reading the PIDs of all the devices with GETPID and then matching it with known PIDS. If it does not match, then it will attempt to grab a mem slab for i3c_device_desc. It will then obtain the standard I3C information from the device.

briefdescription.para: Allocate memory for a i3c device descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This allocates memory from a mem slab for a i3c_device_desc

briefdescription.para: Free memory from a i3c device descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This frees memory from a mem slab of a i3c_device_desc

briefdescription.para: Report if the i3c device descriptor was from a mem slab.

detaileddescription.para: This reports if the i3c_device_desc was from a memory slab

simplesect.para: True if from a memory slab, False if not

briefdescription.para: Allocate memory for a i3c i2c device descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This allocates memory from a mem slab for a i3c_i2c_device_desc

simplesect.para: Pointer to allocated i3c_i2c_device_desc, NULL if none available

briefdescription.para: Free memory from a i3c i2c device descriptor.

detaileddescription.para: This frees memory from a mem slab of a i3c_i2c_device_desc

briefdescription.para: Report if the i3c i2c device descriptor was from a mem slab.

detaileddescription.para: This reports if the i3c_i2c_device_desc was from a memory slab

simplesect.para: True if from a memory slab, False if not

briefdescription.para: Fallback submit implementation.

detaileddescription.para: This implementation will schedule a blocking I3C transaction on the bus via the RTIO work queue. It is only used if the I3C driver did not implement the iodev_submit function.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for an I3C controller driver.
Prepared submissions queue entry connected to an iodev defined by I3C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE.

briefdescription.para: Submit request(s) to an I3C device with RTIO.

parameterdescription.para: Prepared submissions queue entry connected to an iodev defined by I3C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE.

memberdef.argsstring: (struct rtio *r, struct rtio_iodev *iodev, const struct i3c_msg *msgs, uint8_t num_msgs)
memberdef.name: i3c_rtio_copy

briefdescription.para: Copy the i3c_msgs into a set of RTIO requests.

parameterdescription.para: RTIO IODev to target for the submissions
Number of i3c msgs in array
Last submission in the queue added
Not enough memory in the context to copy the requests

briefdescription.para: Max and min Open Drain timings.

detaileddescription.para: Standard I3C SDR and I2C FM speed

briefdescription.para: Structure initializer for i3c_device_id from PID.

detaileddescription.para: This helper macro expands to a static initializer for a i3c_device_id by populating the PID (Provisioned ID) field.

briefdescription.para: iterate over all I3C devices present on the bus

parameterdescription.para: an I3C device descriptor pointer updated to point to the current slot at each iteration of the loop

briefdescription.para: iterate over all I2C devices present on the bus

parameterdescription.para: an I2C device descriptor pointer updated to point to the current slot at each iteration of the loop

briefdescription.para: safely iterate over all I3C devices present on the bus

parameterdescription.para: an I3C device descriptor pointer updated to point to the current slot at each iteration of the loop
an I3C device descriptor pointer used as a safe temp variable

briefdescription.para: safely iterate over all I2C devices present on the bus

parameterdescription.para: an I2C device descriptor pointer updated to point to the current slot at each iteration of the loop
an I2C device descriptor pointer used as a safe temp variable

memberdef.name: I3C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: const struct i3c_iodev_data _i3c_iodev_data_##name = { \ .bus = DEVICE_DT_GET(DT_BUS(node_id)), \ .dev_id = I3C_DEVICE_ID_DT(node_id), \ }; \ RTIO_IODEV_DEFINE(name, &i3c_iodev_api, (void *)&_i3c_iodev_data_##name)

briefdescription.para: Define an iodev for a given dt node on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: These do not need to be shared globally but doing so will save a small amount of memory.

parameterdescription.para: Symbolic name of the iodev to define

briefdescription.para: Define an iodev for a devicetree instance on the bus.

detaileddescription.para: This is equivalent to I3C_DT_IODEV_DEFINE(name, DT_DRV_INST(inst)).

parameterdescription.para: Symbolic name of the iodev to define

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for Improved Inter-Integrated Circuit (I3C) controllers.
